data.content: The problem is the file compilation order in the `.fsproj`. In F#, files can only reference types/modules from files listed **above** them. `Services.fs` depends on `Domain.fs`, but is listed first.
data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. In F#, file compilation order matters — files can only reference modules/types defined in files that appear *before* them in the project file. 

In the `.fsproj`, `Services.fs` is listed before `Domain.fs`, but `Services.fs` depends on `Domain.fs` (it opens `BrokenOrder.Domain`). The fix is to reorder so `Domain.fs` comes before `Services.fs`.

index 0000000..0000000 100644
--- a/tmp/sv-ec034c3e08b642aeb7416f6b95768f01/fsharp/BrokenOrder/BrokenOrder.fsproj
+++ b/tmp/sv-ec034c3e08b642aeb7416f6b95768f01/fsharp/BrokenOrder/BrokenOrder.fsproj
@@ -4,8 +4,8 @@
     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
-    <Compile Include="Services.fs" />
     <Compile Include="Domain.fs" />
+    <Compile Include="Services.fs" />
     <Compile Include="Program.fs" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content: Build succeeded. The fix was swapping the compile order in `BrokenOrder.fsproj` so that `Domain.fs` is listed before `Services.fs`. In F#, file order matters — each file can only reference modules defined in files listed above it in the project file. `Services.fs` depends on `BrokenOrder.Domain`, so `Domain.fs` must come first.
